Hazard Level (Inferred): Very High (highest frequency of structural failure in greening trees)
Diagnostic Warning Points: Yellow / golden primordial stage in early summer; often associated with Pagoda trees

Hazard Level (Inferred): High (causes severe heart rot and structural hollowing)
Diagnostic Warning Points: Cocoa-brown spore powder covering the cap surface; turns dark brown when bruised

Hazard Level (Inferred): High (Brown Root Rot disease; causes rapid death)
Diagnostic Warning Points: Thick brown mycelial mat on roots; black network lines in wood
Presence of black rhizomorphs and white mycelial fans under bark

Hazard Level (Inferred): High (causes major structural loss via brown cubical rot)
Diagnostic Warning Points: Brown, velvety surface; common on Larch roots

Hazard Level (Inferred): High (can kill trees and cause root failure)
Diagnostic Warning Points: Black crust-like appearance, easily peels from bark when young (grayish)

Hazard Level (Inferred): Medium (causes heart rot but structural failure varies)
Diagnostic Warning Points: Bright salmon pink to chrome orange shelf-like clusters

Hazard Level (Inferred): Medium (strong wood-decay power but usually on branches/sapwood)
